title: A New Fossil Ape Changes Where We Think Humans Came From
summary: Scientists found a new fossil ape in Egypt that is about 17-18 million years old. This fossil, called Masripithecus, helps us learn more about where apes and humans started. Before, many scientists thought early apes came from East Africa, but this discovery suggests they might have come from northern Africa instead. The fossil shows that apes were diverse and spread to other places long ago. This finding is important because it tells us we need to look in new places to understand human origins better. It reminds us that science can change with new discoveries, and there is still much to learn about our past.

questions.question: What do scientists study to learn about origins?

background_read: This article is about a fossil discovery that helps scientists understand where apes and humans came from. Fossils are old bones or imprints left by animals that lived long ago. Scientists use fossils to learn about life in the past. Apes are animals like gorillas and chimpanzees, and humans are also a type of ape. This new fossil was found in Egypt and is very old, about 17-18 million years. It suggests that early apes might have started in northern Africa, not East Africa as many thought before. This shows how new discoveries can change what we know and encourage scientists to explore more places.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists and researchers who found a fossil ape in Egypt
WHAT: A new fossil called Masripithecus that is 17-18 million years old and suggests apes might have started in northern Africa
WHERE: Northern Egypt, in the Wadi Moghra region
WHY: To learn more about where apes and humans came from and to challenge old ideas about human origins

perspectives.description: This perspective focuses on how new fossils can change our understanding of history and encourage more exploration.
